'''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|ja|梶山古墳}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|ja|1981年}} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Sagiyama Kofun - Designated on November 27, 1981<ref name="Tottori Prefecture">[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument Sagiyama Kofun] (Tottori Prefecture Cultural Properties Navigator).</ref>.

'''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|ja|梶山古墳}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|ja|1981年}} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>

'''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|ja|梶山古墳}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|ja|1981年}} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>

'''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|ja|梶山古墳}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|ja|1981年}} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>

== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|ja|梶山古墳}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|ja|1981年}} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>

revision.comment: moving the kofun template to the bottom of each page
revision.text:
'''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|ja|梶山古墳}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|ja|1981年}} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Sagiyama Kofun - Designated on November 27, 1981<ref name="Tottori Prefecture">[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument Sagiyama Kofun] (Tottori Prefecture Cultural Properties Navigator).</ref>.
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

'''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|ja|梶山古墳}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year }} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Sagiyama Kofun - Designated on November 27, 1981<ref name="Tottori Prefecture">[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument Sagiyama Kofun] (Tottori Prefecture Cultural Properties Navigator).</ref>.

'''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|ja|梶山古墳}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year }} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Sagiyama Kofun - Designated on November 27, 1981<ref name="Tottori Prefecture">[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument Sagiyama Kofun] (Tottori Prefecture Cultural Properties Navigator).</ref>.

Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|ja|梶山古墳}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year }} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Sagiyama Kofun - Designated on November 27, 1981<ref name="Tottori Prefecture">[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument Sagiyama Kofun] (Tottori Prefecture Cultural Properties Navigator).</ref>.

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|ja|梶山古墳}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year }} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Sagiyama Kofun - Designated on November 27, 1981<ref name="Tottori Prefecture">[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument Sagiyama Kofun] (Tottori Prefecture Cultural Properties Navigator).</ref>.

'''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}}. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年|12=draft}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|ja|梶山古墳|12=draft}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year|12=draft}} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>

'''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}}. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年|12=draft}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|ja|梶山古墳|12=draft}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year|12=draft}} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>

'''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}}. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年|12=draft}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|ja|梶山古墳|12=draft}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year|12=draft}} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Sagiyama Kofun - Designated on November 27, 1981<ref name="Tottori Prefecture">[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument Sagiyama Kofun] (Tottori Prefecture Cultural Properties Navigator).</ref>.

[[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]] [[Category:Kofun in Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tottori City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Tottori Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Decorated Tombs]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E9%B7%BA%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E9%B3%A5%E5%8F%96%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]] [[ja:鷺山古墳]] [[sn:agiyama Kofun (Tottori)]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

'''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県|12=draft}}. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年|12=draft}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|ja|梶山古墳|12=draft}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀|12=draft}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|ja|1981st year|12=draft}} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Sagiyama Kofun - Designated on November 27, 1981<ref name="Tottori Prefecture">[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument Sagiyama Kofun] (Tottori Prefecture Cultural Properties Navigator).</ref>.

9 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:鷺山古墳 (鳥取市) up to rev 101009753
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 鷺山古墳 |画像 = [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), entrance.jpg|280px]]
石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[鳥取県]][[鳥取市]]国府町町屋（字鷺山） |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 28|緯度秒 = 44.85 |経度度 =134|経度分 = 16|経度秒 = 46.72 |ISO = JP-31 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径10m
高さ2m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]（中高式） |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 鳥取県指定史跡「鷺山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = [[装飾古墳]] |地図 = Japan Tottori |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 鷺山古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''鷺山古墳'''（さぎやまこふん）は、[[鳥取県]][[鳥取市]]国府町町屋にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。鳥取県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 鳥取県東部、稲葉山から南に延びる尾根上で甑山の北側に築造された古墳である。[[1966年]]（[[昭和]]41年）に発見されている{{Sfn|町屋村（平凡社）|1992}}{{Sfn|鷺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 墳形は円形で、直径約10メートル・高さ約2メートルを測る{{Sfn|鷺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。玄室天井の中央部を高く構築する中高式の石室であるが、玄室天井石と羨道部は欠失する{{Sfn|鷺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。壁面には魚・船・鳥形文や幾何学文様など多数の線刻画が認められており、特に奥壁中央に描かれた魚形文は体長1.2メートルを測る巨大な魚（[[サケ]]または[[マス]]か）で、目・口・背びれ・尾びれなどを写実的に描く点で特色を示す{{Sfn|鷺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。魚形文は[[梶山古墳]]・空山古墳群でも知られるが、全国的には珍しい地方色の強い文様として注目される「空山古墳群」『鳥取県の地名』日本歴史地名大系32、平凡社、1992年。。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]末頃と推定される{{Sfn|鷺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 古墳域は[[1981年]]（昭和56年）に鳥取県指定史跡に指定されている。 Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|玄室奥壁の線刻画（魚形文） == 文化財 == === 鳥取県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 鷺山古墳 - 1981年（昭和56年）11月27日指定[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument 鷺山古墳]（鳥取県ホームページ「とっとり文化財ナビ」）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（鳥取県教育委員会、1984年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=町屋村|title=鳥取県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]32|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|町屋村（平凡社）|1992}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=津川ひとみ|year=2002|chapter=鷺山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|鷺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument 鷺山古墳] - 鳥取県ホームページ「とっとり文化財ナビ」 {{デフォルトソート:さきやまこふん とつとりし}} [[Category:鳥取県の古墳]] [[Category:鳥取市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:鳥取県指定史跡]] [[Category:装飾古墳]]

石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[鳥取県]][[鳥取市]]国府町町屋（字鷺山） |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 28|緯度秒 = 44.85 |経度度 =134|経度分 = 16|経度秒 = 46.72 |ISO = JP-31 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径10m
高さ2m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]（中高式） |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 鳥取県指定史跡「鷺山古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = [[装飾古墳]] |地図 = Japan Tottori |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 鷺山古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''鷺山古墳'''（さぎやまこふん）は、[[鳥取県]][[鳥取市]]国府町町屋にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。鳥取県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 鳥取県東部、稲葉山から南に延びる尾根上で甑山の北側に築造された古墳である。[[1966年]]（[[昭和]]41年）に発見されている{{Sfn|町屋村（平凡社）|1992}}{{Sfn|鷺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 墳形は円形で、直径約10メートル・高さ約2メートルを測る{{Sfn|鷺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。玄室天井の中央部を高く構築する中高式の石室であるが、玄室天井石と羨道部は欠失する{{Sfn|鷺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。壁面には魚・船・鳥形文や幾何学文様など多数の線刻画が認められており、特に奥壁中央に描かれた魚形文は体長1.2メートルを測る巨大な魚（[[サケ]]または[[マス]]か）で、目・口・背びれ・尾びれなどを写実的に描く点で特色を示す{{Sfn|鷺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。魚形文は[[梶山古墳]]・空山古墳群でも知られるが、全国的には珍しい地方色の強い文様として注目される「空山古墳群」『鳥取県の地名』日本歴史地名大系32、平凡社、1992年。。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]末頃と推定される{{Sfn|鷺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 古墳域は[[1981年]]（昭和56年）に鳥取県指定史跡に指定されている。 Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|玄室奥壁の線刻画（魚形文） == 文化財 == === 鳥取県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 鷺山古墳 - 1981年（昭和56年）11月27日指定[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument 鷺山古墳]（鳥取県ホームページ「とっとり文化財ナビ」）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（鳥取県教育委員会、1984年設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=町屋村|title=鳥取県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]32|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|町屋村（平凡社）|1992}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=津川ひとみ|year=2002|chapter=鷺山古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|鷺山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument 鷺山古墳] - 鳥取県ホームページ「とっとり文化財ナビ」 {{デフォルトソート:さきやまこふん とつとりし}} [[Category:鳥取県の古墳]] [[Category:鳥取市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:鳥取県指定史跡]] [[Category:装飾古墳]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]][[ja:鷺山古墳 (鳥取市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q113498608]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu Town, Tottori City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in [[:en:Kajiyama Kofun|Kajiyama Kofun]] and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], during the later [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Sagiyama Kofun - Designated on November 27, 1981<ref name="Tottori Prefecture">[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument Sagiyama Kofun] (Tottori Prefecture Cultural Properties Navigator).</ref>.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * Historical Site Explanation Board (Tottori Prefectural Board of Education, 1984) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=Machiya Village|title=Tottori Prefecture Place Names|series={{ill|Japanese Historical Place Names|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Place Names|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 32|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Hitomi Tsugawa|year=2002|chapter=Sagiyama Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument Sagiyama Kofun] - Tottori Prefecture Cultural Properties Navigator

It was discovered in [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.

The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], during the later [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]][[ja:鷺山古墳 (鳥取市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q113498608]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in [[:en:Kajiyama Kofun|Kajiyama Kofun]] and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], during the later [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>

The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]][[ja:鷺山古墳 (鳥取市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q113498608]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in [[:en:Kajiyama Kofun|Kajiyama Kofun]] and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], during the later [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Sagiyama Kofun - Designated on November 27, 1981<ref name="Tottori Prefecture">[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument Sagiyama Kofun] (Tottori Prefecture Cultural Properties Navigator).</ref>.

The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]][[ja:鷺山古墳 (鳥取市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q113498608]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in [[:en:Kajiyama Kofun|Kajiyama Kofun]] and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], during the later [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>

The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]][[ja:鷺山古墳 (鳥取市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q113498608]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in [[:en:Kajiyama Kofun|Kajiyama Kofun]] and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], during the later [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>

The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in [[:en:Kajiyama Kofun|Kajiyama Kofun]] and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], during the later [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]][[ja:鷺山古墳 (鳥取市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]][[ja:鷺山古墳 (鳥取市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]][[ja:鷺山古墳 (鳥取市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q113498608]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Tottori Prefecture|Tottori Prefecture]]. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in [[:en:1966|1966]] (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in [[:en:Kajiyama Kofun|Kajiyama Kofun]] and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late [[:en:6th century|6th century]], during the later [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in [[:en:1981|1981]] (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]][[ja:鷺山古墳 (鳥取市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]][[ja:鷺山古墳 (鳥取市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]][[ja:鷺山古墳 (鳥取市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q113498608]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}}. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|en|Kajiyama Kofun|WD=Q11538863|lt=Kajiyama Kofun}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981}} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Sagiyama Kofun - Designated on November 27, 1981<ref name="Tottori Prefecture">[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument Sagiyama Kofun] (Tottori Prefecture Cultural Properties Navigator).</ref>.

'''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture}}. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|en|Kajiyama Kofun|WD=Q11538863|lt=Kajiyama Kofun}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981}} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>

Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.

It was discovered in {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
