The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|en|Kajiyama Kofun|WD=Q11538863|lt=Kajiyama Kofun}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981}} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.

== Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Sagiyama Kofun - Designated on November 27, 1981<ref name="Tottori Prefecture">[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument Sagiyama Kofun] (Tottori Prefecture Cultural Properties Navigator).</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]][[ja:鷺山古墳 (鳥取市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]][[ja:鷺山古墳 (鳥取市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori)]][[ja:鷺山古墳 (鳥取市)]]
'''Sagiyama Kofun''' (鷺山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja|鳥取市国府町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu Town, Tottori City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|en|Tottori Prefecture|WD=Q133935|lt=Tottori Prefecture|1=Tottori Prefecture}}. Its shape is a circular tomb (円墳). The kofun is designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture.
== Overview == [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Unfolded view of the stone chamber}}]] The Sagiyama Kofun was constructed on a ridge extending southward from Mount Inaba, north of Mount Koshi, in eastern Tottori Prefecture. It was discovered in {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966|1=1966}} (Showa 41){{Sfn|Machiya Village (Heibonsha)|1992}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The tumulus has a circular shape, measuring approximately 10 meters in diameter and 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The burial facility is a {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the south. The chamber features a middle-high structure with the central part of the ceiling raised, but the ceiling stones of the main chamber and the dromos are missing{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The chamber walls are adorned with numerous engraved motifs, including fish, boats, birds, and geometric patterns. Notably, the rear wall of the chamber features a large fish-shaped engraving, measuring 1.2 meters in length, which depicts realistic details such as eyes, mouth, dorsal fin, and tail, likely representing a salmon or trout{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>. Fish-shaped engravings are also found in {{ill|Kajiyama Kofun|en|Kajiyama Kofun|WD=Q11538863|lt=Kajiyama Kofun|1=Kajiyama Kofun}} and the Soraoyama Kofun Group, but they are rare nationwide, reflecting strong regional characteristics<ref>"Soraoyama Kofun Group" in ''Tottori Prefecture Place Names'', Japanese Historical Place Names Series 32, Heibonsha, 1992.</ref>. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed in the late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}, during the later {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}{{Sfn|Sagiyama Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}.
The kofun was designated as a historic site by Tottori Prefecture in {{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981|1=1981}} (Showa 56)<ref name="Tottori Prefecture"/>.
<gallery> Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-1-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the rear wall) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-2-2.jpg|Main chamber (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-2.jpg|Dromos (towards the entrance) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), sendou-1.jpg|Dromos (towards the main chamber) Sagiyama Kofun (Tottori), genshitsu-3.jpg|Engravings on the rear wall of the main chamber (fish-shaped engraving) </gallery>
== Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Sagiyama Kofun - Designated on November 27, 1981<ref name="Tottori Prefecture">[http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/B8FB4B54B387BC334925796F0007FE5F?OpenDocument Sagiyama Kofun] (Tottori Prefecture Cultural Properties Navigator).</ref>.
